title: Manhattan U.S. Attorney Announces Charges Against Leaders Of 'OneCoin,' A Multibillion-Dollar Pyramid Scheme (DOJ, SDNY)
bias_or_interest: Government charging announcement. Authoritative for the charges, the MLM mechanics, and the official 'pyramid scheme' characterization (allegations).
relevant_locations: Charges: wire fraud, securities fraud, money laundering conspiracies
'multibillion-dollar pyramid scheme' framing (title)
MLM: commissions for recruiting; educational packages + tokens to 'mine'
quotation_note: Allegations — attribute to the indictment/prosecutors; Ignatova charged not convicted. justice.gov blocks automated fetch (403); confirm verbatim in a browser before air.
notes: Spine of the charges movement. Cross-check SRC-0003 (IRS-CI verbatim mirror).

title: Co-Founder Of Multibillion-Dollar Cryptocurrency Scheme 'OneCoin' Sentenced To 20 Years In Prison (DOJ, SDNY — Greenwood)
bias_or_interest: Government release on a PROVEN guilty plea (co-founder Greenwood). Firmest basis for 'no real blockchain', 'fake coins', and the scheme's fraudulent nature.
relevant_locations: Greenwood guilty plea (2022) and 20-year sentence (2023-09-12)
No genuine blockchain; central database; supply/price set centrally
Internal emails prosecutors cite ('a story we can sell to the members'; 'take the money and run')
quotation_note: Greenwood's plea = admitted facts (stateable as 'prosecutors say / the court found'); Ignatova's intent remains ALLEGED. Verify ★ quotes verbatim on DOJ page.
notes: Load-bearing for 'no blockchain'. Cite the SDNY trial record (US v. Mark Scott, 18-cr-318) for exhibit emails on-screen.

title: Bulgarian Woman Charged for Role in Multibillion-Dollar Cryptocurrency Pyramid Scheme OneCoin (IRS-CI mirror of DOJ release)
bias_or_interest: IRS-CI reproduces the DOJ release verbatim; used to confirm the scale figures fetchable (DOJ blocks bots).
relevant_locations: '€4.037 billion in sales revenue' (Q4 2014–Q4 2016); 'profits of €2.735 billion'
'victims invested over $4 billion worldwide'
'over three million people invested'; founded 2014 in Sofia by Ignatova & Greenwood; MLM
quotation_note: Figures are the government's (OneCoin's own books, per DOJ) — attribute 'prosecutors say / OneCoin records show'. Verbatim-confirmed this research pass.
notes: Primary mirror for the scale numbers.

title: Former Law Firm Partner Sentenced To 10 Years In Prison For Laundering $400 Million Of OneCoin Fraud Proceeds (DOJ, SDNY — Mark Scott)
bias_or_interest: Government release on a PROVEN jury conviction (2019) and sentence (2024). Safe to state plainly.
relevant_locations: Mark Scott convicted 2019 of money laundering (~$400M via fake 'Fenero Funds')
Sentenced 10 years (2024-01-25); ~$393M forfeiture
quotation_note: Conviction is fact. Appeal: 'has appealed' — do NOT say conviction 'upheld' (not confirmed against a primary court record).
notes: Anchors 'related convictions are proven in court'.

title: Co-Founder Of Multi-Billion-Dollar Cryptocurrency Pyramid Scheme 'OneCoin' Pleads Guilty (DOJ, SDNY — Konstantin Ignatov)
bias_or_interest: Government release; Konstantin Ignatov (Ruja's brother) guilty plea + cooperation. PROVEN.
relevant_locations: Konstantin Ignatov took over leadership after Ruja vanished
Arrested LAX 2019; guilty plea (fraud + money laundering); cooperating witness
quotation_note: Do NOT cite the '90-year maximum' as his sentence (cooperating witness, not sentenced in these sources).
notes: His testimony (Scott trial) re the blockchain was later challenged on other matters — attribute carefully.

title: Ruja Ignatova Added to FBI's Ten Most Wanted Fugitives List (FBI)
bias_or_interest: FBI. Authoritative for the Most Wanted listing and the fugitive status.
relevant_locations: Added to Ten Most Wanted 2022-06-30
FBI assumes she is still alive (SA Paul Roberts, 2022)
quotation_note: Procedural facts stateable directly. fbi.gov blocks bots (403); confirm in a browser.
notes: Hosts event footage clip. Cross-check SRC-0007 for reward.

title: Reward for Information: German National Ruja Ignatova (U.S. Department of State)
organization: U.S. Department of State — Transnational Organized Crime Rewards Program
bias_or_interest: State Dept reward page. Authoritative for bio, the travel, and the reward amount.
relevant_locations: German national, born in Bulgaria (Ruse, 1980-05-30)
Traveled Sofia -> Athens on/about 2017-10-25; whereabouts unknown
Reward up to $5,000,000 (raised from $250,000 in June 2024)
quotation_note: Bio + travel + reward stateable. Fate unknown — do not assert.
notes: Firmest primary for the bio and the reward escalation.

title: OneCoin Ltd (Dubai) / OneLife Network Ltd (Belize): cease and desist (BaFin)
bias_or_interest: German regulator order. Independent contemporaneous warning/action (2017).
relevant_locations: Order to 'dismantle their internet based OneCoins trading system' and end German sales promotion immediately (2017-04-27)
Earlier froze the payment processor's accounts (Feb 2017)
quotation_note: Verbatim-confirmed this pass. A real regulator acting BEFORE the collapse = 'they were warned'.
notes: Pairs with the UK FCA warning (SRC-0009/0010) for the regulator-chorus beat.

title: Cryptoqueen: How this woman scammed the world, then vanished (BBC News, Jamie Bartlett)
author: Jamie Bartlett
bias_or_interest: Major-outlet investigation. Source for the Wembley 'Bitcoin killer' pitch, the FOMO/believer dynamic, the FCA-warning-removal reporting.
relevant_locations: Wembley (June 2016): 'Bitcoin killer'; 'in two years, nobody will speak about Bitcoin any more'
FOMO + recruiting commissions = true believers who silenced skeptics
UK FCA warning later removed (~2017), reportedly after lawyer pressure
quotation_note: Attribute pitch quote + dynamics to 'the BBC's reporting / Jamie Bartlett'. Removal-motive = reported, not proven.
notes: The narrative engine for the seduction movement.

title: The Missing Cryptoqueen (BBC podcast, Jamie Bartlett & Georgia Catt)
author: Jamie Bartlett
bias_or_interest: Landmark BBC reporting that brought the case to a global audience; source for victim accounts (Jen McAdam) and Bjercke.
relevant_locations: Released Sept 2019; global reporting milestone
Jen McAdam (victim/advocate); Bjorn Bjercke (no-blockchain account)
quotation_note: Credit the BBC; the documentary's title 'Nothing' is distinct but the case popularization is the BBC's.
notes: Cite for victim dignity + the technologist account.

title: Norwegian Blockchain Dev Says OneCoin Uses SQL Instead of Blockchain (Bjorn Bjercke)
author: Bjorn Bjercke (interviewed)
bias_or_interest: Technologist account: OneCoin tried to recruit him to BUILD a blockchain it did not have; ran on an SQL database.
relevant_locations: OneCoin had no blockchain; sought a dev to build one
Coins stored/assigned via SQL database
quotation_note: Expert/whistleblower opinion — attribute ('the developer says'); corroborates SRC-0002 (Greenwood plea).
notes: Color for the 'no blockchain' beat; not a court finding on its own.

title: Compensation for Victims of OneCoin's Cryptocurrency Investment Fraud (FBI)
bias_or_interest: FBI victim page — underscores the scale of real, identifiable victims.
relevant_locations: Victim-compensation/claims process; millions of victims worldwide
quotation_note: Dignity-first; victims are real and the moral center.
notes: Pairs with SRC-0015 (2026 claims process) for the coda contrast.

title: OneCoin (Wikipedia)
bias_or_interest: Neutral aggregator; corroborates 'own database rather than a blockchain, no mining process', timeline, structure.
relevant_locations: 'maintained its own database of coins rather than using a blockchain and had no mining process'
Founded 2014, Sofia; MLM; timeline
quotation_note: Corroboration only; cross-checked vs DOJ/IRS-CI primaries.

title: Ruja Ignatova (Wikipedia)
bias_or_interest: Neutral aggregator; bio corroboration (PhD Konstanz; reported Oxford/McKinsey; persona).
relevant_locations: PhD private international law, University of Konstanz (2005)
Reported Oxford / McKinsey background; 'Dr Ruja' persona
quotation_note: Treat Oxford/McKinsey as 'publicly reported'; Konstanz PhD firmest. Cross-check SRC-0007.

title: U.S. DOJ Opens Claim Process for Victims of OneCoin's $4 Billion Fraud (CoinDesk)
bias_or_interest: Recent reporting on the DOJ victim-claims process; the seized-asset fund is a fraction of the ~$4B lost.
relevant_locations: 2026 DOJ victim-compensation claims process (~$40M fund vs ~$4B lost)
quotation_note: Use for the coda contrast (recovered «« lost). Verify the fund figure before air.
